City of Alameda California


                                                    APR 23 1991


                            April 22, 1991


Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814-0807


     Re:  Request for Formal Written Advice for Councilmember
          Karin Lucas

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     Ms. Karin Lucas was recently elected to the City Council of
the City of Alameda.  In that capacity, she also serves on the
board of the Community Improvement Commission (the "CIC"), which
is the City's redevelopment agency.  On behalf of Ms. Lucas, I am
writing to request formal written advice pursuant to Government
Code section 8314(b) regarding the ability of Ms. Lucas to
participate in decisions regarding the City's proposed
redevelopment plan.

     The City is in the process of adopting a redevelopment plan
for Business and Waterfront Improvement Project.  A public hearing
on the adoption of the proposed Plan has been scheduled for May 21
before the CIC, and therefore we would appreciate your response as
soon as possible.  The question is whether Councilmember Lucas may
participate in the hearing and vote on the adoption of the proposed
redevelopment plan.

     The Business and Waterfront Redevelopment Area includes the
major older commercial and industrial areas of the City.  Overall,
the proposed area comprises about 10% of the City's total area and
54% of the City's commercial and industrial area.  In order to
accomplish comprehensive revitalization of the older commercial and
industrial areas of the City, the proposed redevelopment area
includes only areas currently designated for commercial/industrial

     Office of the City Attorney, Room 314

     City Hall
     Santa Clara Avenue at Oak Street · 94501
     415.748.4544

Hallabrin
April 22, 1991
Page 2


uses, and virtually no residential areas.  A copy of a map of the
proposed project area is enclosed for your information.  The
proposed plan includes public improvement projects such as
commercial building rehabilitation and facade improvements,
transportation improvements, storm drainage improvements, curb and
gutter improvements, public parking improvements, expansion of
civic facilities, recreational open space and low and moderate
income housing improvements.

     Councilmember Lucas owns a lot developed with two buildings
in front and a garage building in back at 2522 and 2254 Encinal
Avenue.  The lot is located 200 feet from the westernmost boundary
of the proposed project area, west of the intersection of Encinal
and Oak.  On the first floors of the two buildings in the front,
there are a total of three professional office suites.  On the
second floor of each building there is one apartment.  Major
remodelling was completed in 1987.  The parking on the property
complies with the zoning requirements.

     The property is zoned R-6.  The R-6 zoning permits a mix of
homes, professional offices, institutional and tourist oriented
uses.  A copy of the municipal code regulations for the R-6 zone
are attached.  It should be noted that the Municipal Code allows
professional offices out right in the R-6 zone but restricts the
location of professional offices in the commercial and industrial
zones.  A copy of that ordinance is attached.

     Across the street from the property, the whole block is
occupied by Alameda High School.  According to Ms. Lucas her
property is already developed to its highest and best use, and has
ample off-street parking.  The 2200 block of Encinal is dominated
by the high school across the street and adjacent residential use.
In her opinion, it would be unrealistic to expect changes in this
block from the redevelopment plan which is intended to improve
commercial and industrial areas.

     A copy of the detailed map of the project area indicating the
location of Ms. Lucas' property is enclosed along with photographs
of the area.  With the one exception of one vacant parcel on the
other side of town, there is no residentially zoned property within
the proposed project area.

     As I mentioned above, the City Council and the CIC will be
holding a joint public hearing on the proposed redevelopment plan

Hallabrin
April 22, 1991
Page 3


on May 21, and we would therefore appreciate a response as quickly
as possible.  If you need further information, please do not
hesitate to call me at (415) 748-4544.


                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [signature]
                                          Heather C. McLaughlin
                                          Assistant City Attorney

required side yard for each story above the first story
of any building, but shall not be required to be more
than ten feet. The side yard on the street side of a
corner lot shall be not less than ten feet.

(g)    Rear Yard: Twenty feet. Not more than 40 percent of the
rear yard, as defined in Section 11-1246, may be occupied
by accessory buildings or structures (swimming pools
excepted).  (As amended by Ordinance No.  1533 N.S.,
effective August 12, 1966.)

(h)    Distances Between Main Buildings on Same Lot: 20 feet.
(As amended by Ordinance No. 2363 N.S., effective
October 15, 1987.)

       (1)    (Repealed by Ordinance No.  2363 N.S., effective
              October 15, 1987.)

       (2)    (Repealed by Ordinance No.  2363 N.S., effective
              October 15, 1987.)

       (3)    (Repealed by Ordinance No.  2363 N.S., effective
              October 15, 1987.)

       (4)    (Repealed by Ordinance No.  2363 N.S., effective
              October 15, 1987.)

       (5)    (Repealed by Ordinance No.  2363 N.S., effective
              October 15, 1987.)

(i)    Off-Street Parking and Loading Space: As regulated in
Article 4-C of these Regulations.

(j)    Usable Open Space Requirements: The usable open space,
as defined in Section 11-14A12, on each building site
shall be not less than 200 square feet for each dwelling
unit.  (As added by Ordinance No. 1757 N.S., effective
April 18, 1975.)

R-6, HOTEL RESIDENTIAL DISTRICT REGULATIONS

Sec.  11-1321.   GENERAL.   The following  specific
regulations and the general rules set forth in Article 4-A shall
apply in all R-6 Districts, as delineated and described in the
Zoning Map(s).  It is intended that this district classification
be applied in areas where one and two-family homes and a mix of
professional office, institutional and tourist-oriented uses may
be located to promote low density residential standards and a mix
of more intense business uses.  (As amended by Ordinance No.
2363 N.S., effective October 15, 1987.)


                              -406-

NOV  1 1988

Sec. 11-1322. USES PERMITTED.

(a) Uses permitted in the R-1, R-2, R-3, R-4 and R-5
Districts.

(b) Hotels, motels, hospitals, rest homes, professional
offices for doctors, dentists, architects, engineers,
accountants, artists, authors, attorneys, real estate and
insurance offices, medical and dental clinics, and other
uses which are similar to the foregoing; and clubs,
lodges and fraternities, except those open to the general
public or operated as a business or for profit, adminis-
trative office of nonprofit community social service
organizations. (As amended by Ordinance No. 1844 N.S.,
effective February 4, 1977.)

(c) Incidental and accessory buildings and uses on the same
lot with, and necessary for, the operation of any per-
mitted use.

(d) Signs: Those pertaining directly to a permitted multiple
family or non-residential use on the property, and as
further regulated in Article 4-B of these Regulations.

(e) Bed and breakfast facilities, upon compliance with
standards set forth in Section 11-127.1. (As added by
Ordinance No. 2267 N.S., effective February 7, 1986.)

Sec. 11-1323. USES REQUIRING USE PERMIT. It is the
intent of the Section that the following uses shall be reviewed
by the Planning Board for their propriety in a specific location
or for such other factors as safety, congestion, noise, and
similar considerations:

(a) Temporary tract sales offices, advertising signs, con-
struction offices, equipment storage yards or structures
therefor, which are incidental to the development during
the construction and/or sales period.

(b) Satellite dish antennas, subject only to those
restrictions necessary to protect adjacent properties
from unsightly or obtrusive structures. (As amended by
Ordinance No. 2289 N.S., effective July 25, 1986.)

Sec. 11-1324. MINIMUM HEIGHT, BULK AND SPACE
REQUIREMENTS.

(a) Lot Area: Not less than 5,000 square feet in all lots
recorded on and after the effective date of this Chapter,
August 1, 1958. However, regardless of date of

-407-

NOV 1987

recordation, there shall be not less than five hundred
(500) square feet of lot area for each dwelling unit, and
not less than three hundred fifty (350) square feet of
lot area for each bedroom in a hotel, rooming boarding
house, residence club or motel.
(As amended by Ordinance
No. 2363 N.S., effective October 15, 1987.)

(b)     Lot Width: Not less than 50 feet on all lots recorded on
and after the effective date of this Chapter, August 18,
1958. (As amended by Ordinance No. 1476 N.S., passed
August 18, 1964.)

(c)     Maximum Main Building Coverage: 60 percent of lot area.

(d)     Building Height Limit: 8 stories, but not to exceed 100
feet.

(e)     Front Yard: 20 feet.

(f)     Side Yards: Side yards shall total not less than twenty
percent of the lot width, and no side yard may be either
less than five feet or be required to be more than ten
feet.     However, two feet shall be added to each required
side yard for each story above the first story of any
building. The side yard on the street side of a corner
lot shall be not less than ten feet.

(g)     Rear Yard: Twenty feet. Nor more than 40 percent of the
rear yard, as defined in Section 11-1246, may be occupied
by accessory buildings or structures (swimming pools
excepted). (As amended by Ordinance No. 1533 N.S.,
effective August 12, 1966.)

(h)     Distances Between Main Buildings on Same Lot: Same as
required in R-5 Districts.

(i)     Off-Street Parking and Loading Space: As regulated in
Article 4-C of these Regulations.

(j)     Usable Open Space Requirements: The usable open space,
as defined in Section 11-14A12, on each building site
shall be not less than 120 square feet for each dwelling
unit. (As added by Ordinance No. 1757 N.S., effective
April 18, 1975.)

AP, ADMINISTRATIVE-PROFESSIONAL DISTRICT REGULATIONS

Sec. 11-1325. GENERAL. The following specific regu-
lations and the general rules set forth in Article 4-A shall
apply in all AP Districts, as delineated and described in the

-408-

NOV 1 1987

CITY OF ALAMEDA ORDINANCE NO. 2416
New Series

AMENDING CHAPTER 1, TITLE XI (ZONING) OF THE MUNICIPAL CODE
RELATING TO ADMINISTRATIVE, PROFESSIONAL AND OTHER OFFICES,
AND MEDICAL AND RELATED FACILITIES.

BE IT ORDAINED by the Council of the City of Alameda that:

SECTION 1. The Alameda Municipal Code is hereby amended by adding
Section 11-1326(a)(11) of Article 3 of Chapter 1 of Title XI thereof to read:

Sec. 11-1326. Uses Permitted.

(a) (11)Optometrists

SECTION 2. The Alameda Municipal Code is hereby amended by amending
Section 11-1330(b) of Article 3 of Chapter 1 of Title XI thereof to read:

Sec. 11-1330. Uses Permitted.

(b) The following retail and similar business uses when conducted
principally within enclosed structures and where said uses are not
doing business between the hours of 10:00 p.m. and 7:00 p.m.

SECTION 3. The Alameda Municipal Code is hereby amended by adding
Section 11-1330(b)(18) of Article 3 of Chapter 1 of Title XI thereof to read:

Sec. 11-1330(b)(18). Uses Permitted.

(18) Uses permitted in the AP District, and other office uses not
associated with permitted retail sales use of the site, provided that
for any frontage of a building adjoining a public street, public alley or
public sidewalk, 50 percent in depth of the ground floor space nearest
such frontage shall be reserved for retail sales and/or service uses
permitted in the C-1 District.

SECTION 4. The Alameda Municipal Code is hereby amended by amending
Section 11-1331(b) of Article 3 of Chapter 1 of Title IX thereof to read:

Sec. 11-1331(b). Uses Requiring Use Permits.

(b) Uses permitted in the AP District, and other offices uses not
associated with permitted retail sales use of the site, which are not
otherwise permitted in Section 11-1330(b)(18).

SECTION 5. The Alameda Municipal Code is hereby amended by amending
Section 11-1334(b)(10)of Article 3 of Chapter 1 of Title XI thereof to read:

Sec. 11-1334(b)(10). Uses Permitted.

(10) Uses permitted in the AP District, and other office uses not
associated with permitted retail sales use of the site, provided that
for any frontage of a building adjoining a public street, public alley or
public sidewalk, 50 percent in depth of the ground floor space nearest
such frontage shall be reserved for retail sales and/or service uses
permitted in the C-2 District.

1

I, the undersigned, hereby certify that the foregoing
Ordinance was duly and regularly adopted and passed by
the Council of the City of Alameda in regular meeting
assembled on the 7th of February, 1989,
by the following vote to wit:

AYES:  Councilmembers Arnerich, Thomas and President
       Corica - 3.

NOES:  None.

ABSENT:Councilmembers Camicia and Monsef - 2.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and
affixed the official seal of said City this 8th day of
February, 1989.


_________________________________
City Clerk of the City of Alameda

SECTION 6. The Alameda Municipal Code is hereby amended by amending
Section 11-1335(f) of Article 3 of Chapter 1 of Title IX thereof to read:

Sec. 11-1335(f). Uses Requiring Use Permits.

(f) Uses permitted in the AP District, and other office uses not
associated with permitted retail sales use of the site, which are not
otherwise permitted in Section 11-1334(b)(10).

SECTION 7. This Ordinance shall be in full force and effect from and after
the expiration of thirty (30) days from the date of final passage.


C. J. CORICA
Presiding Officer of the Council

Attest:

[illegible] City Clerk
CITY ATTORNEY

2
